title: 10 best herbal teas for nail fungus

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Germander tea contains antifungal compounds such as germanderin and limonene, which inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, helping to prevent further infection.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Germander tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as geranial and neral, soothe and calm irritated skin and nails, reducing redness and swelling associated with fungal infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teucrium chamaedrys, also known as germander, teas helps with nail fungus because of its antifungal and antiseptic properties. The herb contains compounds like teucrin and teucrin B, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, including those causing nail fungus. Additionally, germander's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. This makes Teucrium chamaedrys a promising natural remedy for treating fungal nail infections.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which could help combat fungal infections in nails.
Catechins: A type of flavonoid that may inhibit fungal growth and reduce inflammation, potentially aiding in the treatment of nail fungus.
Volatile terpenes (e.g., borneol, camphor): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which could help prevent fungal overgrowth and promote a healthy environment for nail growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Teucrium chamaedrys for making teas due to their high content of essential oils that exhibit antifungal properties.
Roots: Roots are used for their medicinal properties, including antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help in treating nail fungus.
Stems: Stems are also used due to their ability to act as a source of essential oils that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, aiding in the treatment of nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried teucrium chamaedrys flowers and leaves for every 8 ounces of water needed.
Steep the dried flowers and leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste for sweetness if desired to mask bitter flavors.
Serve the herbal tea hot or iced and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Elder tea contains antifungal compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in elder tea can help protect the nail bed from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, teas helps with nail fungus because of its antifungal properties. The Elderberry plant contains flavonoids and anthocyanins that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, including nail fungus. These compounds work by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, ultimately leading to the death of the fungus. As a result, the tea can help to promote healthy nail growth and reduce the appearance of fungal infections, such as yellowing or thickening of the nails. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antifungal properties, which can inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton rubrum.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of fungal infections in the nail.

Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth and potentially promoting the healing of nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their high content of anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a compound called saponin, which has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity and help treat nail fungus.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of isothiocyanates, which have been found to have antifungal and antibacterial properties that aid in treating nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-50 grams of dried sambucus nigra berries from a reputable source or harvest your own.
Measure and combine the dried berries with 250-500 ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before serving and consuming in moderation due to potential interactions.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fungal infections under the nails.

Antifungal properties: The tea's antifungal compounds, such as luteolin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species, including those that cause nail fungus, by disrupting their cell membranes and inhibiting their metabolic pathways.

By drinking Echinacea purpurea tea, individuals may be able to stimulate their immune system and reduce inflammation, creating an environment less conducive to fungal growth and potentially promoting healthy nail growth. Flowers: The flowers are used because of their reported antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Gently harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from mature plants.
Crush 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers and leaves into a powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Serve the tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes for optimal benefits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency and flavor.
Rinse the harvested herbs in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Chop the rinsed herbs into small pieces using kitchen shears or a sharp knife within 1 minute.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh strainer into a cup and serve immediately after 30 seconds.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nail fungus that are treated with herbal teas are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin. Teas like green tea and turmeric also contain antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nail fungus are a natural remedy that helps alleviate fungal infections on the nails. These teas contain antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi, promoting healthy nail growth. Examples of herbal teas for nail fungus include tea tree oil, oregano, and turmeric, which have been shown to improve symptoms and reduce the risk of infection recurrence. Improved nail health can boost confidence and overall well-being, enabling individuals to engage in daily activities with ease and comfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with nail fungus is a blend of tea tree oil-infused peppermint tea, echinacea tea, and calendula tea. Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties combat fungal infections, while peppermint tea's cooling effect soothes the nail bed. Echinacea tea's immune-boosting properties support the body's natural defenses, and calendula tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and promote healing. Drinking this blend can help alleviate symptoms and promote healthy nail growth.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Journal of Family Practice, researchers conducted a double-blind, multicenter, randomized controlled trial involving 117 patients with distal subungual onychomycosis. The participants were divided into two groups, one receiving 1% clotrimazole solution and the other 100% Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree) oil, applied twice daily for 6 months. The study found that the tea tree oil group had a slightly higher rate of culture cure (18%) and similar rates of clinical improvement (60%) compared to the clotrimazole group, suggesting that herbal teas, particularly tea tree oil, may be a viable option for treating nail fungus due to their potential efficacy and tolerability.
